We arrived 15 minutes before closing and were told we could only order one dish, even though my young daughter was starving.
We already had other food to take back to the hotel, yet we were repeatedly met with complaints about the restaurant closing.
I understand closing procedures, but the attitude and manners were unacceptable.
Beautiful place and great food, but very poor service.

Poor service, food is extremely bland and you can’t even change it because they have a policy that the chef’s recipe cannot be changed. Well they need to tell their chef to actually cook good food in the first place then.

The worst experience of my life. The waiter was all jokes no humor and felt like he was looking down upon us. First off when you reserve a table it doesn’t mention which place you are reserving it. We were already caught off guard when that happened and then the waiter was the worst. Overall would never go back again

My favorite pizza in New York city paper thin puffy crust with a little bit of crunch in a lot of chew is so delicious when paired with the arugula, lemon vinaigrette, shaved Parmesan salad together it’s an outstanding combination

Justo luego de realizar el recorrido del museo del 9/11, muy cerca de allí está este super restaurante. También tiene una zona de compras, pero aquí lo importante es su restaurante, de excelente servicio, muy buena selección de pastas y vinos. Como el restaurante esta en el último piso del edificio, tiene una muy buena vista de la ciudad. Super Ultra Recomendable!
We always love coming here when we’re in town. Never disappoints and the food and service are always great!

It used to be goog when it opened. I went yesterday for caffe with panna, was served a burned espresso with bad whipped cream. Cakes look old.
Go have your pastries and coffee at San Ambreus at the Oculus, much better than this store, with a bad coffee and old pastries.

Eataly NYC at the Oculus has, unfortunately, become a consistently disappointing destination, with quality that seems to decline with every visit. For a venue positioned as a premium Italian food hall in such a prominent and expensive location, the overall experience falls well short of expectations.
Food quality and execution. Also the pizza arrived cold.
The pizza is presented on a  metal plate, covered by paper. Which feels more cafeteria than curated Italian dining and does not align with the upscale image or pricing of the establishment. The dough itself is chewy and lacks depth of flavor, weak execution for a dish that should be a cornerstone of any serious Italian offering.
Burrata
The burrata, traditionally prized for its soft, creamy center, was served unusually firm and dense, closer in texture to a standard mozzarella than to a fresh burrata. For a brand that markets itself on high-quality Italian ingredients, serving a burrata in this condition raises concerns about both product handling and quality control.
Value versus expectations
Given the premium pricing and the high-traffic World Trade Center / Oculus location, guests are entitled to expect a higher standard of culinary consistency and presentation. Instead, the experience feels misaligned with the cost, leaving the impression of a concept trading on its name
Leider das schlechteste Pizza, welche wir gegessen haben. Vom verbrannten Teig waren auch die Zutaten geschmacklos. Ich rate es nicht.

I went out with my gf the waiter saw we were younger did not provide proper service at all and FORCED A 20 dollar tip as we tried to give only 10 because the service was horrendous also the food was not that good either
So many choices. Just amazing. Great place for coffee and desert.

Eataly is an incredible Italian food destination that blends dining, shopping, and culture all in one place. The quality of ingredients really stands out—from fresh pasta and wood-fired pizza to imported cheeses, cured meats, and excellent espresso. The market is fun to browse, with so many authentic Italian products and wines you won’t find everywhere.

The restaurants and food counters offer something for every craving, whether you want a full sit-down meal or a quick bite. It can get busy and prices are on the higher side, but the freshness, authenticity, and overall experience make it worth it. A must-visit for Italian food lovers.
Killer gelatos

Came specifically for this location, only to find a sign at the entrance saying “Closed due to private event.”
No advance notice on Google Maps, no website update, no warning until you physically arrive at the door.

This is not a small café — Eataly is a destination venue. Closing for a private event is something planned in advance, and customers should not have their time wasted like this.

If you plan to shut down regular service, at least communicate it clearly online.
Very disappointing experience and poor customer consideration.

Disappointing Experience with Management

Before we even had a chance to sit down and enjoy our food, we experienced extremely poor customer service at the main Eataly restaurant on the 2nd floor at 101 Liberty St, New York, NY 10007.

It was around 4 PM on a Monday 12/15/2025 not a busy time at all. When we exited the elevator, the restaurant and hostess stand were directly in front of us. A manager standing there immediately yelled, “Stay right there, I’ll come to you” no greeting, no welcome.

We were a group of 7 people (4 adults, 1 teen, and 2 small children). She insisted we should go to Al Pasta & Al Pizza because they have a kids menu and the main restaurant does not. I explained that we know the menu well and that it would still work for us, and that we were fine ordering regular pasta for the kids. She kept repeating that there is no kids menu, even after I clearly said that was not an issue for us.

Shortly after, an older couple walked in and were seated immediately, while we were told to wait. While seating them, the manager looked at me and pointed at my stroller and said, “Can you put that thing over there?” That was the final straw for me. We decided to leave.

We went to Al Pasta & Al Pizza, which is also part of Eataly but on the other side, and the experience there was the complete opposite. The hostess was warm and kind, greeted all of our kids, and you could tell she genuinely loves children. Our waitress was wonderful, and the food and service were excellent.

When we were leaving and walking back toward the elevator, I wanted to ask for the manager’s name. The hostess said she could not give it to me and called her instead. When I politely asked for her name, she again was rude and refused to share it, saying she manages multiple locations. I find it unacceptable for someone in a management position to refuse to identify themselves when asked.

I have lived in New York for many years and have never experienced this type of behavior, nor am I someone who leaves negative reviews, this is actually my first one. I’m sharing this so families with kids are aware. Based on this experience, this location, or at least the management we encountered, is clearly not kid-friendly…

Eataly New York is a great place to experience authentic Italian food in the heart of the city. During our visit, we tried several traditional dishes and everything was delicious.
We tasted different pizzas, which were well cooked, flavorful, and made with high-quality ingredients. The dough was light and crispy, just like in Italy. We also had rustic bread served with olive oil, which was excellent and very fragrant, with a rich and genuine taste.
Another highlight was the Italian-style potatoes, simple but perfectly prepared, with a homemade flavor that reminded us of traditional Italian cuisine. The quality of the ingredients really stood out in every dish.
The atmosphere is lively and welcoming, and the place is well organized despite being quite busy. It’s a perfect spot for a casual meal or to try real Italian flavors while visiting New York.
So, offers an authentic Italian food experience with excellent quality and a warm atmosphere. Highly recommended for anyone who loves Italian cuisine.
Just a fun spot to grab dinner or grab some good Italian food to prep at home!

I tried to order a Cannolo with cherries and orange (their special).

Out of orange — so I agree to just cherries. They try to ring it up as something different (and 50 cents extra). So they want to charge me extra because they’re out of an ingredient.
Best Tiramisu
Food is delicious and our waiter Ronnie is exceptional. Wonderful atmosphere and beautiful views.
Ronnie was the best...  ever!
We had the most amazing server RONNIE!!!!

Eataly is an absolute must-visit! From the moment I walked in, the hospitality was top-notch specially by Ronny one of the people who made the night. What really blew me away was the personal touch – they sent a birthday card! It’s these little things that make a huge difference. And let’s not forget the food; every single bite was amazing and delicious. The quality of the ingredients shines through in every dish. If you're looking for an authentic Italian experience with incredible service and food, Eataly is the place to go. Highly recommended! I had the capricciosa pizza , which is a must go if you like anchovies ‼️
Beautifully decorated and the food was out of this world!

Eataly at World Trade Center is an easy win when you’re craving a quick pasta fix without committing to a full sit-down meal. For an elevated food-court setting, the execution is excellent: the pasta is freshly made, well-sauced, and consistently flavorful, delivering that comforting Italian simplicity Eataly does so well.

Portions are on the smaller side, but thoughtfully so—just enough to satisfy without leaving you overly full, making it ideal for a lunch break or a pre-errand bite downtown. Everything feels efficient and polished, from ordering to pickup, which matters in a high-traffic location like WTC.

The only real drawback is the price. While the quality justifies it to an extent, it does feel a touch expensive for what is, at the end of the day, an upscale food-court experience.

Final take: Excellent pasta, smart portions, and reliable quality in a convenient location—just a bit pricey. ★★★★☆

Update: No one has ever reached out to me to address the situation.

I’m very disappointed with my experience at Eataly this Thanksgiving. The service was especially poor compared to my visits last year. Our server was barely present. She took our order at the beginning and then disappeared until it was time to bring the bill. No one checked on us at all.

It took 40 minutes just to receive the antipasti with the first course, and during that entire time, no staff member came by to check in or update us. We simply sat there waiting in silence, unsure of what was happening.

Meanwhile, another staff member was constantly refilling water for every table around us but consistently skipped ours. I understand he may not have been responsible for our section, but this only added to the feeling of being ignored, especially considering the restaurant wasn’t even busy.

The food also fell short of expectations. The beet with walnut antipasti tasted off; the walnuts were oily, as if they had been sitting out for too long. The Brussels sprouts were heavily burnt, black, bitter, and unpleasant. Although they offered to replace the side dish, the second serving wasn’t much better, as shown in the photo.

I’m genuinely disappointed that what should have been a warm Thanksgiving celebration turned into such an unsatisfying experience. I hope the restaurant can take this feedback seriously.
Fun to walk around and see the great variety of foods.  We did drinks and appetizers.
David and Maria at the pizza place bar are amazing! Great service. Really love the vibe 🫶🏻🫶🏻🫶🏻🫶🏻

The experience at Eataly World Trade Center was amazing. From the service to the food I was super impressed. I’ve never eaten authentic Italian cuisine so this was new to my taste buds. The atmosphere was restaurant dine-in and a fresh market together. So it was a bit nosier than most restaurants, but we could still hear each other. The area where we ate was a tad bit tight but still comfortable. I ordered two pasta dishes, butter squash, ravioli and just regular spaghetti no meat. Both were a phenomenal. I’m not sure exactly what was the name of the dishes of the pasta or the dessert? Hopefully, the pictures will help.

Wonderful piece of Italy in America! I’m so jealous of New Yorkers that have this gem of a giant marketplace of rich authentic, and rare Italian products (at least rare food in America).

Highly recommend their Vino & restaurant. Book ahead for one of the best pizza spots and pastas freshly made daily. Also, by booking you get a GREAT view of the city! I just had the best calzone of my life there.

My wife is an Italian and as an Italian speaker myself we both love this place. It’s also often a pleasure to speak Italia to the staff here. We just stopped in for my wife’s birthday, where they even comped us the dessert!
The service quality was high, the food was delicious, and the atmosphere was great. I highly recommend it to everyone.

If you are coeliac or have any allergies, DO NOT visit this chain!

When entering, I mentioned I was coeliac. I asked the waiter if I could have the pizza, and they said, "No, we really wouldn't recommend the pizza for coeliacs, but the pasta is safe for coeliacs", I therefore asked if the gluten-free pasta was cooked in separate pasta water from the normal pasta, and I was assured it was.

Within 20 minutes of eating my meal, I was ill. I had to go to the toilet, I felt nauseous, I had cold sweats, I was lightheaded, and I had severe abdominal cramps. I had 100% been given/contaminated by gluten.

I messaged their Instagram page upon my return to double-check that the pasta is cooked separately when gluten-free. I was told it is not and that it is not safe for coeliacs.

I made the company aware of the issues I had and explained how dangerous this was, and the waiter assured me the food was safe for me.

The company said I would be contacted by email or phone call to discuss further, which was on the 16th of October.
On the 27th October, I contacted the company to ask whether I would be getting a phone call or email, and they responded apologising for the delay and that no further time would be wasted before they contact me.
On the 3rd November, I asked for an update, and I still (as of 4th December) have not received a response or further communication from this company.

They clearly do not take allergies or coeliac disease seriously in this establishment, so AVOID!

Leider kann ich gar keine gute Rezension für diesen Betrieb abgeben. Das Personal ist unglaublich gestresst und ich musste mehrmals nachfragen, wenn ich etwas wollte. Die Portionen sind extrem klein und die Verhältnisse sehr teuer. Es ist sehr län ick. Eine typische Massenabfertigung in einem Supermarkt. Dies mag wohl für die Leute aus New York passen aber für Touristen definitiv nicht kein guter Ort um hinzugehen.

The service was ok but I ordered veggie omelet homefries toast the omelet tasted horrible and weird made me feel sick and the utensils looked dirty with white spots all over them. The only thing that tasted ok was the homefries, toast and hot chocolate. I will never be going back there again.

Nach unserem ersten Frühstück im ZAZA waren wir sofort begeistert – und kamen gleich mehrmals wieder. Die Karte ist vielfältig, dazu gibt es immer auch saisonale Spezialitäten. Besonders überzeugt haben uns die Qualität der Speisen und die frischen, liebevoll angerichteten Salatbeilagen.

We went there for our brunch and we loved the food. The service is also really good compared to other places we went.

Great breakfast! Our omelets with skillet home fries were large portions, very good flavor and reasonably priced. The fries had sauteed onions and green peppers in them - which was a pleasant surprise. Nice, large and comfortable tables and space.
Really great food & Christmas ambiance, the Christmosa was FAB 💯

We came to this dinner at NY and I was great we ordered a French toast for the baby it was full of flavor. My wife a chicken sandwich it was good but I ordered the pasta I tried the zaza but it was a little tasteless. I had to add tabasco to be able to add some kind of flavor. Overroll it was great

Really impressed, and I'm a breakfast diva.

We waited less than 10 minutes for a table for 4 in the middle of brunch rush. The positive energy the staff brings is exactly what we needed to start a packed day of walking and sightseeing.

Boozy coffee and hot chocolate for Mom and Dad, Milkshakes for the kids.  Food portions were the size of a jet ski.  I didn't expect there to be so many good choices for breakfast/brunch in this neighborhood, and was really pleasantly surprised.

And only about 25% more than bringing the family to our townie diner at home.

Locale curato, pulito e tranquillo, cosa non scontata a New York soprattutto al mattino. Ambiente piacevole, servizio veloce e staff molto gentile, ti fanno sentire subito a tuo agio.
Colazione abbondante e ben fatta, porzioni generose e presentazione curata. Prezzi alti se confrontati con gli standard italiani, ma perfettamente in linea con la zona e con il tipo di locale. Nota positiva: fanno uno sconto del 5% pagando in contanti, dettaglio che non guasta.
In sintesi, un posto affidabile per iniziare bene la giornata. Ci tornerei senza problemi.
The Service was perfect. Cathy made the best service
Katherin is so kind person. We loved to spend time there and with her.

We had a wonderful dining experience. We started with eggs Benedict with salmon — absolutely delicious — followed by perfectly cooked fried salmon with creamy mashed potatoes. We also tried the omelets, along with coffee, lattes, and cappuccinos. Everything was exceptionally tasty.

The portions are very generous — truly filling, to the point that we felt satisfied for almost the entire day. The atmosphere is warm, cozy, and welcoming, making the experience even more enjoyable.

The restaurant is very clean, the water is crystal clear, and the service is impeccable. Plates are cleared promptly, even while you are still dining, without being intrusive.

Overall, everything is done at a very high level. We will definitely be happy to return.
Every time we came in NY we stop by food always good

Had the cheesesteak eggs benedict which was on their seasonal menu, very tasty and the portions were excellent. Make sure you get here early on the weekends to beat the line, was about a 10-15 minute wait at around 10:30 on a Sunday morning. Would revisit to try more things off the menu
What can I say, a great place.. Staff are excellent, food great... Came here 15th Dec. Very Christmasy, very nice relaxed atmosphere
First time visiting from the UK and the experience was amazing. All staff were friendly and welcoming.
My favorite brunch spot, their dinners are good as well. Reasonable prices, good food, friendly service.
Service was friendly and attentive, and the staff checked in regularly to make sure everything was good. Even though the place was busy, the food came out quickly.
Great friendly staff, great fast service, clean environment, delicious menus options, plenty of seating! I enjoyed the Monday special “chicken parm” and it was delicious!!! Perfect lunch option!
The best roasted potatoes and onions I’ve ever had. The breakfast sandwich was incredible. Wouldn’t hesitate to come back.

Went in without any big expectations, and wow, was I pleasantly surprised. The staff were super attentive without being over the top, and the food… wow. Everyone at our table loved what they ordered. I got the chicken milanese, and it was cooked perfectly—crispy on the outside, tender inside. Honestly, it’s rare to find a place where every single dish hits the mark, but this one did. Totally casual, totally satisfying, and made the whole meal feel easy and fun. Definitely adding this one to my “go back” list.

Great experience. Perfect meal, just as ordered, and wasn’t even on the normal breakfast weekday menu but they insisted to make it anyways. Very unique and cool atmosphere. Great staff and management.
Thank you Cheyenne!

We had an extra night in NYC due to blizzard and after visiting The Smith we were definitely not sad of the extra time we spent there.
Kind staff, cosy atmosphere and great food.
I recommend their Monday special “Peppercorn burger”.

Top!

Muy buena atención, personal muy amable y atento. La comida deliciosa. El lugar es muy agradable y con nivel de ruido que permite hablar cómodamente.
We had drinks at the bar! Very nice bartender and overall staff, huge variation of drinks available.

We first came in for brunch and the food was fine. The goat cheese omelet was solid, but I liked the greens and vinaigrette on the side more than the omelet. The breakfast sandwich was really good, and the potatoes that came with it were the best part of the brunch. The potato waffle was bland, but they have salt and pepper on the table.

Flash to a couple nights later after a terrible experience at a late-1800's "themed" bar down the street, and The Smith saved our evening. They were able to sit us quickly after we left the prior establishment (given we didn't have a reservation), and their bar was large enough that sitting there while waiting was easy.

Dinner:
Truffle Arancini Appetizer - solid choice, but I'd have liked more of the white sauce.
Half Chicken - the sauce was punchy and was perfect for the chicken and the veggies were cooked perfectly. It wasn't a true half-chicken, but I honestly think it was better because of that. Instead, it was a large breast and a leg; I think this choice kept it from being dried out the way half chickens can be.
French Dip - the au jus was thick and savory, and the bread was amazing. Everything on the sandwich melded together in a great way.
Sticky Toffe Pudding - I've never had such a perfect dessert from a restaurant. It was amazing in every way.

On top of it all, service was great; our waitress, Chloe, was a doll. This was a great experience for us.
The manager stopped to check on us multiple times during our visit. Staff and food very good. I recommend this place.
Ein sehr schönes Restaurant mit leckeren Speisen und großen Portionen! Sehr nette Bedienung! Wir würden jederzeit wieder kommen.😊

Classic diner experience with great service. Not quite the best pancakes I have had, but close.
Delicious avocado toast, really good cheesecake. Warm atmosphere!

This is not your ordinary diner.  When you walk in you’re warmly greeted with a smile.  As I was being escorted to a booth I noticed how light and spacious the diner felt.  It is tastefully decorated ahead of the holidays.  From my booth I noticed two televisions featuring different stations, but neither competing for sound over the other.  After seating, I was given enough time to look over the menu.  The timing to order and needs while eating were anticipated — you can’t teach anticipation nor top-notch service.  I ordered the French Toast with a side of Canadian Bacon and Scrambled Eggs with Cheese.  As much as I wanted to eat all of my tasty breakfast I could not eat all of the French Toast.  Be aware the portions are ample for the price you pay.
Food is always great!
Great place to eat with the family. Good food, large portions and the prices were very good for NYC. The food is delicious. The portions are super big!

Sehr toller Diner mit vielfältiger Karte und unterschiedlichen Gerichten für jeden Geschmack. Portionen voll ausreichend und sehr, sehr wohlschmeckend. Zwei Tage jeweils Frühstück getestet, absolut empfehlenswert und preislich in Ordnung.
Personal extrem freundlich.
Unbedingt hingehen.

Das Zaza Restaurant ist wirklich ein Geheimtipp!
Die Pancakes mit Apple Cinnamon Crumble waren einfach himmlisch – fluffig, perfekt gewürzt und mit diesem feinen Apfel-Zimt-Crunch ein echtes Highlight. Auch der Avocado Toast mit den scrambled Eggs war mega lecker, frisch zubereitet und wunderbar abgeschmeckt. Der Saft schmeckte herrlich frisch und der Kaffee war ebenfalls top – kräftig, aber ausgewogen im Geschmack.

My friends and I had a wonderful time there. The ambience is nice and the restaurant is big! The food is absolutely delicious! We had the braised short rib mafaldine and the burger royale.  We can’t wait to go back during a girls’ night out.

Definitely a good place for fresh oysters. Food is pretty good. Great for families.
Alex G was fantastic, we had a lovely Valentine’s Day brunch

Le Bernardin delivers classic French fine dining with a strong focus on seafood. The dishes are beautifully presented and highlight very fresh, high-quality ingredients. The lobster cappuccino was a standout and a big hit with our group. I would have loved to see a few more meat options, but overall it’s an elegant experience and a great choice for anyone who enjoys refined seafood cuisine.

Wanted to love this so badly as it was a bucket list experience for us. The food was very good, but simply not worth the price tag.

It made me think about what I would expect out of a meal that costs $1000 for two (even without the wine pairing), and what this meal was missing which made it feel so expensive for what it was.

The two things for me were 1) innovation + diversity in courses and 2) complexity in flavor.

The tasting menu started out really strong, but the last 4 savory courses (out of 8) were slabs of white fish or prawn accompanied by varying sauces. The flavors didn’t feel new or very layered/complex. A series of straightforward, solid dishes that mainly rely on the ingredients to shine.

I could see this place being fantastic for those that typically don’t love seafood, or are stopping by for lunch.

The dessert and service were fantastic though!

Dining at Le Bernardin was truly exceptional. Every dish was phenomenal, but the tuna with foie gras was particularly memorable - perfectly balanced, luxurious, and beautifully executed. The fish throughout the meal was cooked with incredible precision; each course showcased such delicacy and care that it was clear we were experiencing something very special.

The bread course was also amazing—thoughtfully presented and absolutely delicious, setting the tone for the rest of the evening.

What elevated the experience even further was the service. It was easily some of the best we have ever had. Lehman, Marcos, and Michael were wonderful—warm, attentive, knowledgeable, and genuinely gracious. They made the entire evening feel seamless and welcoming.

We left genuinely impressed by how beautifully everything was prepared and by the extraordinary hospitality. Thank you for such a memorable meal.

I am visiting from Colorado, and my friend was visiting from Texas. My parents are regular visitors of this restaurant. My parents told me that we could go sit at the bar and we don’t need reservations. I called the Restaurant at 4 PM and asked if we could come in and sit at the bar… They said absolutely “yes…No reservations needed.”

Restaurant opens at 5 PM. We got there at 6 PM. We were told that someone bought out the wine bar and that the restaurant was not able to accommodate anyone in the lounge or the bar. There were three open seats at the bar, but apparently they were now “Reserved.”

Either a restaurant has principles and directions on what they do and what they don’t do… For example, you do not need reservations at the bar and it’s all open seating. Or that’s not the case. Either way, the person answering the phone should’ve known that the wine bar was bought out and that they were going to reserve seats at the bar… Or they should not have reserved seats at the bar and followed their principles and let anyone walk in.  That is not what is expected from a three Michelin star restaurant

Anyway, we had a great dinner at a neighboring restaurant in the vicinity. I’ve been to many Michelin star restaurants… I doubt I will try to go back here.

The Good
The dining room is elegant without being oppressive. It strikes that careful balance between refinement and comfort — though parts of it feel just slightly dated, like a luxury hotel that hasn’t refreshed the carpet since its last Michelin ceremony.
Most dishes were beautifully presented. Plates arrived composed and deliberate, each element clearly told where to stand. The cold crab cake was excellent — pristine, delicate, and disciplined. The lobster appetizer showcased sweet, tender lobster that deserved applause.
My daughter’s Peruvian chocolate cake was divine — rich, layered, and unapologetically indulgent. It briefly restored faith in the trajectory of the evening.

The Bad
The tomato broth accompanying the lobster appetizer was aggressively salted. Not enhanced. Not seasoned with confidence. Salted.
The mushroom pasta, while flavorful, lacked the elegance one expects at this level. Presentation matters here.
The main-course lobster in red wine sauce suffered from the same heavy hand. My daughter’s pasta entrée followed suit. By the midpoint of the meal, we began to anticipate the sodium more than the seafood. The pattern was unmistakable: excess salt, consistently applied.
My own dessert, in contrast to my daughter’s triumph, was immensely forgettable — the culinary equivalent of elevator music.

The Ugly
The bread service was the evening’s most surprising miscalculation.
Three varieties were offered. The croissant-like brioche was respectable — soft, buttery, and the sole member of the basket that met expectations.
The focaccia was dry and hard rather than crusty, as though it had been holding a reservation of its own since the afternoon. The petite baguette was indistinguishable from a competent grocery store offering. I have had better bread in diners — and I do not say that lightly.
The assortment of candies presented at the end of the meal was similarly underwhelming. Perfectly edible. Entirely ordinary. At this level, ordinary feels extravagant in the wrong direction.

Final Thought
When dining at this level, one hopes for restraint, refinement, and quiet confidence — not a heavy-handed reminder that salt, like power, is best used judiciously.

The staff runs a tight ship—courteous, quick, and efficient, which makes the whole experience smooth. The menu itself is huge, so no matter what you’re in the mood for, you’ll find something. The place is also kept spotless, and you can tell they take pride in it—just check out how clean their restrooms are.

Portions here are generous, so you won’t be leaving hungry. And the coffee? Easily some of the strongest I’ve ever had—exactly how I like it in the morning. I haven’t tried lunch or dinner yet, but judging by the menu, I’d bet they’re just as solid.

Location is also a plus—it’s just steps from the World Trade Center. If you’re in the area, I definitely recommend stopping by.
